rulings.Case Title: PEGGY JOHNSON et al VS. RENELLA ANDERSON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 29, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFFS PEGGY JOHNSON and L. GIBSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff fails to shift the burden of proof on the issue of whether plaintiff Gibson is disabled for purposes of the rent ordinance. The letter from SSA are inadmisible hearsay and Gibson's declaration lacks sufficient foundation. The motion for summary adjudication is Granted as to the 10th, 17th, and 19th affirmative defenses as unopposed. Granted as to the 14th affirmative defense because plaintiffs concede that all the actions in the complaint are based on events that occurred after 2/8/09. Denied as to all other affirmative defenses, failure to shift the burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY VS. BURT HAMROL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Answers To Deposition Question And Mtn For Sanctions For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 29, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY'S (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Answers To Deposition Question And Motion For Sanctions For Misuse Of The Discovery Process. GRANTED as to questions #1, 3, 4, 5, 7(first), 7(second), 8 and 9. DENIED as to 2 and 6. Hamrol to appear for 2 hours of deposition within 10 days of notice of this order. Mr. Martin Quinn is appointed discovery referee and is to attend the deposition at a time convenient for all parties. Request for sanctions denied, substantial justification. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FLORENTINO VILLANUEVA VS. GMAC MORTGAGE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gmac Mortgage Llc'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 29, 2010, line 10. Defendant Gmac Mortgage Llc'S Demurrer To Complaint. Overruled as the Second cause of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the First, 3rd. and 4th. causes of action. For the First cause of action (wrongful foreclosure) plaintiff must state facts showing that the foreclosure was wrongful and must allege tender. For the 3rd. cause of action . (Violations of the UCL) plaintiff must allege which prong he asserts the cause of action under and describe with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the alleged violation. For the 4th. cause of action (Quiet Title) plaintiff must allege tender and the title of plaintiff as to which determination is sought. =(302/CWW)
